title: SatForge
description: A from-scratch CDCL SAT solver studio that grew an SMT brain. The SAT core does two-watched literals, VSIDS, first-UIP learning and restarts, emits machine-checkable DRAT proofs of UNSAT and re-verifies them in-browser with an independent RUP/RAT checker, counts models exactly (#SAT, component caching), extracts minimal unsat cores (MUS), and optimizes — a complete weighted MaxSAT engine (linear SAT-UNSAT and core-guided WPM1 over a Generalized Totalizer) plus encoders for N-Queens, Sudoku, coloring, Hamiltonian cycles, integer factoring (a multiplier circuit run backwards) and the zebra puzzle. NEW: a full DPLL(T) SMT solver built on the very same engine — SAT lifted to first-order theories. A lazy DPLL(T) loop abstracts a formula's Boolean skeleton, and on each candidate hands the theory atoms to from-scratch decision procedures: EUF (equality + uninterpreted functions) by proof-producing congruence closure with curried applications and minimal conflict explanations; linear real & integer arithmetic (QF_LRA/QF_LIA) by a general simplex (Dutertre–de Moura) over EXACT δ-rationals — no floating point anywhere — with conflict explanations and integer branch-and-bound; and mixed QF_UFLIA by Ackermann reduction so uninterpreted functions and arithmetic combine soundly without Nelson-Oppen. Ships a tolerant SMT-LIB 2 parser and an SMT Studio with a curated example library (congruence closure, strict ordering cycles, integer pigeonhole, UF+arithmetic) that shows the verdict, the model, and the exact theory lemmas the solver learned to close the search. NEWEST: a complete QF_BV BIT-VECTOR engine by EAGER BIT-BLASTING onto the very same CDCL core — every fixed-width bit-vector operation lowered to a from-scratch hardware circuit (ripple-carry adder, shift-add multiplier, restoring division, barrel shifters for shl/lshr/ashr, signed div/rem/mod, comparators, concat/extract/extend/rotate), so the SAT verdict IS the SMT verdict: sound and complete, no refinement loop. A width-checking SMT-LIB QF_BV parser (let, indexed operators, #b/#x/(_ bvN m) literals), a model decoder (hex/bin/unsigned/signed) that re-checks every model against an independent BigInt reference, and DRAT-CERTIFIED UNSAT — an unsatisfiable bit-vector encoding is re-verified by the existing independent RUP/RAT checker, so the answer is machine-checked. Examples factor integers by running an 8×8 multiplier backwards, prove the XOR-swap and the overflow-free average bit trick, and split signed from unsigned order. NEWEST: a complete QF_AX THEORY OF ARRAYS — McCarthy select/store, constant arrays, and full EXTENSIONALITY — decided without any new theory solver by REDUCING arrays to the EUF + arithmetic already present: read-over-write purification rewrites every select(store(a,i,v),j) into a fresh element pinned by the McCarthy axioms (recursing through nested writes), surviving reads become an uninterpreted function the congruence-closure engine already handles, and array equality is encoded by a Skolem witness index plus saturated-index agreement clauses (the Stump–Barrett–Dill scheme, made finite and ground), so both existing theories inherit arrays for free and QF_ALIA (arrays + integer arithmetic) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned (Array I E) sorts, select/store and ((as const ...) v); the Studio renders the array contents the solver committed to as per-array cell tables. NEWEST: a complete QF_DT THEORY OF ALGEBRAIC DATATYPES — constructors, selectors and testers over lists, trees, pairs, enums and Nat — likewise decided with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING datatypes to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. The reduction is purely additive: it conjoins the free-term-algebra axioms (exactly-one-tester exhaustiveness + disjointness; constructor pinning is_C(C(…)) and sel_i(C(a))=a_i, which yields injectivity for free through selector congruence; the tester link is_C(t) → t = C(sel_1(t),…) instantiated to a bounded selector depth) plus the one genuinely non-EUF ingredient — ACYCLICITY BY INTEGER RANK: a fresh rank:D→Int with rank(t) > rank(child) on every constructor edge, so over a finite set of ground terms a strict > ordering is exactly 'no term is its own subterm' and the existing simplex/branch-and-bound refutes impossible cyclic values like x = cons(a,x). Datatypes thus reduce to QF_UFLIA, Ackermann-combined like UFLIA/QF_ALIA, so QF_DTLIA (datatypes with integer fields) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned declare-datatype/declare-datatypes (mutually recursive), constructor/selector applications and the ((_ is C) t) tester. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, and ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator (every value a real constructor tree, testers reading the real root) that shares no code with the reduction. NEWEST: a complete QF_S BOUNDED THEORY OF STRINGS — the hardest classic theory (unbounded string equations are undecidable), added in the same style with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING strings (up to a length bound L) to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. Each string term becomes a length |s| (the reused str.len symbol) plus L integer code-units str.char$(s,k) — an uninterpreted String×Int→Int function, so EUF gives congruence for free and Ackermann already combines it with the simplex — with padding past the end pinned to a sentinel so a string's value IS its code-units. McCarthy-style per-operator axioms unfold str.++ (concatenation), str.at, str.substr, str.contains/prefixof/suffixof and str.indexof over the ≤L positions into ordinary eq/arith atoms; string equality is kept as an EUF atom AND tied to position-wise code-unit agreement, so '=' means VALUE equality ('ab'·'c' = 'abc'), not term identity. Because the alphabet is constrained only up to equality, the small-model property makes this sound and complete WITHIN the length bound (so x = 'a'·x is refuted by the simplex from |x| = 1 + |x|). The SMT-LIB parser learned the String sort, double-quoted literals and the str.* operators, and the Studio reconstructs each variable's solved text. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator, and ~900 random bounded-string formulas against an independent concrete-string enumerator (plain JavaScript string semantics, sharing no code with the reduction) — part of 267 self-tests in all.
